mapQuestions.question: Which continent is Thailand on?
mapQuestions.answer: Asia, in the south-east, shaped a bit like an elephant's head 🌏

mapQuestions.question: What is the capital of Thailand?
mapQuestions.answer: Bangkok. Thais call it "Krung Thep", which means "City of Angels". Its full ceremonial name is the longest place name in the world, with over 160 letters.

mapQuestions.question: Which countries border Thailand?
mapQuestions.answer: Myanmar (Burma) to the west, Laos to the north-east, Cambodia to the east, and Malaysia to the south.

mapQuestions.question: Has Thailand ever been colonised by Europeans?
mapQuestions.answer: No, never. Thailand is the only country in South-East Asia that was never colonised. Thais are very proud of this, the country's old name "Siam" was changed to "Thailand" in 1939, which means "land of the free".

mapQuestions.question: What ocean and seas touch Thailand?
mapQuestions.answer: The Andaman Sea on the west, the Gulf of Thailand on the east. Both connect to the Indian and Pacific Oceans.

phrases.nativeScript: สวัสดีครับ / ค่ะ

foods.name: Pad Thai
foods.description: Stir-fried rice noodles with prawns or chicken, egg, peanuts, bean sprouts and a sweet-sour-salty sauce. Squeeze a lime over it, life changes.

foods.name: Tom Yum
foods.description: A sour-spicy soup with prawns, lemongrass, lime leaves, galangal and chilli. Smells incredible. Will make you sweat.

foods.name: Mango sticky rice
foods.description: Warm coconut sticky rice with slices of ripe mango on top, drizzled with salty coconut cream. The greatest dessert on earth, fight us.

foods.name: Green curry
foods.description: Coconut milk and green chilli paste with chicken, Thai basil and little round Thai aubergines. Hotter than it looks.

foods.name: Som Tam
foods.description: Green papaya salad pounded in a clay mortar with lime, fish sauce, peanuts and a lot of chilli. The classic Thai street snack.

foods.name: Khao Soi
foods.description: Northern Thai curry noodle soup with crispy noodles on top, lime, pickles and chilli paste on the side. The signature dish of Chiang Mai.

scavengerItems.label: A tiny golden spirit house outside a shop or home
An elephant somewhere, real, statue, or painted on a wall
A garland of marigolds offered at a shrine
A street cart selling mango sticky rice
A cook stir-frying in a wok over a roaring flame
A photo or portrait of the King in a shop or restaurant
A long-tail boat with a wooden propeller shaft
A lotus flower at a temple
A Thai script sign you can't read at all (it's a beautiful alphabet)

geoMatches.description: The capital. Royal palaces, sky-high skyscrapers, floating markets, sky trains, and street food on almost every corner.
The northern city in the mountains. Hundreds of temples, cooler weather, hill tribes nearby, and the home of khao soi noodles.
A big island in the south with turquoise water, hidden coves, and viewpoints over the Andaman Sea.
Thailand's old capital, abandoned in 1767. Now a UNESCO site of crumbling brick temples, with one stone Buddha head wrapped in tree roots.
Towering limestone cliffs rising straight out of the sea, jungle behind, white-sand beaches in front. The setting for many famous films.
The huge north-east region. Rice fields, ancient Khmer temples, the spiciest food in the country, and the warmest welcome.

senses.smell: Lemongrass and chilli from a street wok, jasmine garlands at a shrine, frangipani, charcoal smoke, tropical rain on hot pavement, sometimes durian…
senses.hear: The clatter of woks, scooter horns, "Sawatdee!" from every shop, temple bells, monks chanting at dawn, geckos, the rumble of monsoon thunder…
senses.taste: Sweet, salty, sour and spicy all in one mouthful, sticky rice fingertips, the punch of fish sauce, cooling fresh coconut, mango that tastes like sunshine…
senses.feel: Sticky heat that wraps around you, cool marble at a temple (shoes off), warm sea water, the breeze of a long-tail boat speeding across the bay…
senses.see: What is the most incredible thing right in front of you right now…

stories.question: Why are there little dollhouses outside every building?
stories.body: Those tiny ornate "houses" you see outside almost every Thai shop, home and hotel are called spirit houses. Thais believe each piece of land has its own spirit, and when humans build on the land, the spirits need somewhere of their own to live, otherwise they cause trouble. So they build a small house on a pedestal, decorate it, and leave daily offerings of food, drinks, flowers, even a tiny bottle of strawberry soda. A monk usually chooses the right spot, and the house must face the right way to keep the spirits happy.
stories.thinkingQuestion: If a spirit lived on the land your home is built on, what is one offering you would leave it every day?

stories.question: Why are monks dressed in orange?
stories.body: You will see Buddhist monks everywhere in Thailand, walking in lines at sunrise with bowls, collecting food from people. Their orange robes are not random, the colour comes from a dye that was originally made from boiled jackfruit wood, and it represents simplicity, humility and giving up worldly things. Most Thai boys will spend at least a short time as a monk in their lifetime, sometimes just a week, sometimes years. It is seen as a way of making merit, both for themselves and for their family.

stories.question: What is Songkran?
stories.body: Every April, Thailand celebrates its new year with Songkran, the world's biggest water fight. For three days, the whole country turns into a giant battle of buckets, water pistols and ice-cold hoses. Officially, the water represents washing away the old year. In practice, no one is dry, no one is safe, and it is hilarious. It started as a gentle ritual of sprinkling scented water on the hands of elders. It has grown into something else entirely.
stories.thinkingQuestion: If your country had a national festival, what should it be about, and what would people do?

stories.question: Why is there a Buddha head in a tree?
stories.body: In the ruins of Ayutthaya, the old capital, there is one stone Buddha head completely wrapped in the roots of a fig tree. The story: Ayutthaya was a huge, wealthy city until 1767, when a Burmese army invaded, burned the city to the ground and smashed most of the Buddha statues. One head fell to the ground, and over the next 250 years a tree slowly grew around it, swallowing it into its roots. The tree is still alive, and the Buddha is still inside it.

stories.question: What is Muay Thai?
stories.body: Muay Thai is the national sport of Thailand, a centuries-old kind of kickboxing where fighters use their fists, elbows, knees and shins. It started as the way Thai soldiers were trained for battle, hundreds of years ago. Every fight begins with a slow, graceful dance called the "wai khru ram muay", where the fighter pays respect to their teacher and to the spirits of the ring. Then traditional music plays through the whole fight, getting faster as the action heats up. Thai kids start training as young as 6 or 7, and the best fighters become national heroes.

convoQuestions.question: What's the best thing you've eaten in Thailand?
How spicy is too spicy for you, out of 10?
If you could spend a week as a monk, what do you think the hardest part would be?
What's one thing about how people behave here that is different from at home?
Have you found a favourite temple or place yet? What made it special?
Thais say "mai pen rai" (never mind, it's fine) a lot. Where could you use more of that attitude in your own life?

wordSearch.hint: Thai foods, places and things to spot.

animals.name: Asian elephant
animals.description: Thailand's national animal. At ethical sanctuaries (Elephant Nature Park) you can watch them roam, not ride them.

animals.name: Wat (temple) cat
animals.description: Sleeping on cool tiles in every Buddhist temple courtyard.

animals.name: Long-tailed macaque
animals.description: Lopburi town is overrun with them. Hold onto everything.

animals.name: Tokay gecko
animals.description: Loud, common, found on every guesthouse wall after dark.

animals.name: Indochinese tiger
animals.description: Almost extinct in the wild — fewer than 200 left, mostly in the Western Forest.

animals.name: Hawksbill turtle
animals.description: On the Andaman coast beaches, especially during November–March nesting season.
